### Issue URL (Incorrect Blocking) [https://open.spotify.com/](https://adguardteam.github.io/AnonymousRedirect/redirect.html?url=https%3A%2F%2Fopen.spotify.com%2F) ### Comment > **Problem:** Spotify playback stops during ad breaks when AdGuard is enabled, in Safari only > > **Browsers affected:** Safari > **Browsers NOT affected:** Chromium-based browsers and Dia (The Browser Company) — ads are skipped normally, music keeps playing > > **Description:** > When browsing Spotify Web Player with AdGuard enabled, the 3 ads Spotify plays are blocked correctly in Chromium-based browsers and in Dia, and the music continues playing without interruption. > > However, in Safari, when those same ads would normally play, the music stops completely instead of continuing. The only way to resume playback is to refresh the page. > > **Steps to reproduce:** > 1. Enable AdGuard in Safari > 2. Open Spotify Web Player and start playing music > 3. Wait until an ad break would normally occur > 4. Music stops instead of continuing to the next track > 5. Refresh the page to restore playback > > **Expected behaviour:** > Ads should be blocked and music playback should continue uninterrupted, as it does in Chromium-based browsers and in Dia. ### Screenshots

comment.body:
### Issue URL (Incorrect Blocking) [https://www.twitch.tv/drops/inventory](https://adguardteam.github.io/AnonymousRedirect/redirect.html?url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.twitch.tv%2Fdrops%2Finventory) ### Comment > There appears to be three issues: > > 1) Logging in (https://www.twitch.tv/drops/inventory) is not possible due to the "Your browser is not currently supported." error > 2) On the "All Campaigns" page (https://www.twitch.tv/drops/campaigns), no campaigns are shown > 3) On the "Inventory" page (https://www.twitch.tv/drops/inventory), claiming a drop is not possible due to the "Error Occurred Drop was not claimed." error > > If it helps in diagnosing, the following three custom filter rules appear to resolve all 3 of the above mentioned issues: > > - `@@||k.twitchcdn.net^$stealth=useragent` > - `@@||passport.twitch.tv/protected_login^$stealth=useragent` > - `@@||gql.twitch.tv/integrity^$stealth=useragent` ``` Username: @SharjeelJ ``` ### Screenshots

issue.title: www.higherorlowergame.com

labels.name: A: Problematic
labels.description: Issues, which cannot be resolved easily

issue.body:
# Sorry. The website is infected by AdShield, Inc.

Unfortunately, It is complicated to resolve with a regular adblock rule because the website is being protected by service of Ad-Shield that is an anti-adblock company.

## Userscript Installation
Please block Ad-Shield advertisement by installing [tinyShield userscript](https://github.com/FilteringDev/tinyShield).
Please check its installation guideline written in English by visiting [this URL](https://github.com/FilteringDev/tinyShield/blob/main/README.md).

If you can understand Korean language, please refer https://github.com/FilteringDev/tinyShield/blob/main/README.ko.md.

New change of tinyShield's build script for Apple Safari Extension user (made at 2026 Apr 21) is available.

## Still Have a Problem?
An issue is occurred when the userscript is applied, please report to [this issue tracker](https://github.com/FilteringDev/tinyShield/issues).

comment.body:
> Link is not working. Please write steps to reproduce.

1. 需要一台搭载colorOS的OPPO设备。  
2. 打开设备自带的“天气”APP，点击右上角标志为三个点的按钮。并在随后出现的悬浮框内点击“设置”。  
3. 在新打开的页面里找到“用户体验计划”并打开。  

此时，页面内容将为第1张图片。点击“兴趣管理”，预期将跳转至手机自带的浏览器。随后显示第2张图片的内容。  
关于之前发布的内容，有一点要修改：只需要将先前我所发出的两条规则，同时应用于DNS过滤和AD过滤，即可显示“已禁用”图片的内容。  
p.s.:我不太确定是否强制需要heytap账号。

issue.title: kinovod.pro
issue.body:
### Issue URL (Ads) [https://kinovod.pro/](https://adguardteam.github.io/AnonymousRedirect/redirect.html?url=https%3A%2F%2Fkinovod.pro%2F) ### Comment > ### Problem > An embedded pre-roll video advertisement (Mostbet) appears before movie playback on https://kinovod.pro. The player shows “Advertisement” and a “Skip in 10 seconds” timer. > > ### Steps to reproduce > 1. Open https://kinovod.pro in Safari. > 2. Choose a movie and start playback. > 3. An advertising video plays before the movie. > > ### Expected result > The pre-roll advertisement is blocked. > > ### Additional information > - AdGuard for Mac 2.19.0.2258 > - macOS 26.5.2 > - Safari > - AdGuard Base filter, AdGuard Russian filter, and AdGuard Popups filter are enabled and updated > - HTTPS filtering is enabled > - AdGuard Extra and AdGuard Popup Blocker userscripts are enabled > > The advertisement remains after these settings were enabled. ### Screenshots
Screenshot 1 ![Screenshot 1](https://cdn.adguardcdn.com/sitereports/bjjrtu3hxeogcgcsk4ksgwss08kowo83t0evpx770c.png?nc=1)
### System configuration | Information | Value | |-------------|-------| AdGuard product: | AdGuard for Mac v2.19.0 System version: | `macOS version 26.5.2` Browser: | Safari Filters: | **Ad Blocking:**
AdGuard Base filter

**Annoyances:**
AdGuard Popups filter

issue.body:

issue.title: fix places.googleapis.com
issue.body:
## Prerequisites

- [x] This is not an ad/bug report;
- [x] My code follows the [guidelines](https://github.com/AdguardTeam/AdguardFilters/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md) and [syntax](https://kb.adguard.com/general/how-to-create-your-own-ad-filters) of this project;
- [x] I have performed a self-review of my own changes;
- [x] My changes do not break web sites, apps and files structure.

## What problem does the pull request fix?

- [x] Website or app doesn't work properly;

## What issue is being fixed?

https://github.com/AdguardTeam/AdguardFilters/issues/233814
https://github.com/AdguardTeam/AdguardFilters/issues/233892
https://github.com/AdguardTeam/AdguardFilters/issues/234157
https://www.jbhifi.com.au/ _(Add any item to cart and then checkout)_

Unable to complete checkout process on various websites due to the Google API being blocked for address confirmation due to hiding the referrer from third parties.

Error output from Google API:
```
{
  "error": {
    "code": 403,
    "message": "Method doesn't allow unregistered callers (callers without established identity). Please use API Key or other form of API consumer identity to call this API.",
    "status": "PERMISSION_DENIED"
  }
}
```

Previous attempts to resolve this worked for specifically chemistwarehouse.com.au with #233892 as the URL was:
`https://places.googleapis.com/$rpc/google.maps.places.v1.Places/AutocompletePlaces`

Then to resolve #234157, it was a different URL:
`https://places.googleapis.com/v1/places:autocomplete`

Now just being on jbhifi.com.au, it's become even more randomised with the following URL:
`https://places.googleapis.com/v1/places/xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx`

---

Currently the rule in **allowlist_stealth.txt** is:
`@@||places.googleapis.com/*autocomplete$stealth=referrer`

So to accommodate all three variants, I've modified the rule to allow it to be broader:
`@@||places.googleapis.com^$stealth=referrer`

As per [Google's API docs](https://developers.google.com/maps/documentation/places/web-service/overview), it looks like this domain is used for accepting HTTP requests for this particular purpose, and I do not see why we cannot relax this rule to the places.googleapis.com domain with the stealth modifier.

Alternatively, we could further tighten it with two rules, although if the URL changes again it will require another edit:
`@@||places.googleapis.com/$rpc/google.maps.places.v1.Places/*$stealth=referrer`
`@@||places.googleapis.com/v1/places*$stealth=referrer`

### Terms

- [x] By submitting this issue, I agree that pull request does not contain private info and all conditions are met

comment.body:
At the moment with the current rule already present in **allowlist_stealth.txt**, websites that use the `$rpc` URL will work, including chemistwarehouse.com.au and securelocksmithhighbury.co.uk that you just posted.

It's the newer variants of the URL, which are:
`https://places.googleapis.com/v1/places:autocomplete`
`https://places.googleapis.com/v1/places/xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx` (x's are randomised letters and numbers)

issue.title: fix places.googleapis.com
issue.body:
## Prerequisites

- [x] This is not an ad/bug report;
- [x] My code follows the [guidelines](https://github.com/AdguardTeam/AdguardFilters/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md) and [syntax](https://kb.adguard.com/general/how-to-create-your-own-ad-filters) of this project;
- [x] I have performed a self-review of my own changes;
- [x] My changes do not break web sites, apps and files structure.

## What problem does the pull request fix?

- [x] Website or app doesn't work properly;

## What issue is being fixed?

https://github.com/AdguardTeam/AdguardFilters/issues/233814
https://github.com/AdguardTeam/AdguardFilters/issues/233892
https://github.com/AdguardTeam/AdguardFilters/issues/234157
https://www.jbhifi.com.au/ _(Add any item to cart and then checkout)_

Unable to complete checkout process on various websites due to the Google API being blocked for address confirmation due to hiding the referrer from third parties.

Error output from Google API:
```
{
  "error": {
    "code": 403,
    "message": "Method doesn't allow unregistered callers (callers without established identity). Please use API Key or other form of API consumer identity to call this API.",
    "status": "PERMISSION_DENIED"
  }
}
```

Previous attempts to resolve this worked for specifically chemistwarehouse.com.au with #233892 as the URL was:
`https://places.googleapis.com/$rpc/google.maps.places.v1.Places/AutocompletePlaces`

Then to resolve #234157, it was a different URL:
`https://places.googleapis.com/v1/places:autocomplete`

Now just being on jbhifi.com.au, it's become even more randomised with the following URL:
`https://places.googleapis.com/v1/places/xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx`

---

Currently the rule in **allowlist_stealth.txt** is:
`@@||places.googleapis.com/*autocomplete$stealth=referrer`

So to accommodate all three variants, I've modified the rule to allow it to be broader:
`@@||places.googleapis.com^$stealth=referrer`

As per [Google's API docs](https://developers.google.com/maps/documentation/places/web-service/overview), it looks like this domain is used for accepting HTTP requests for this particular purpose, and I do not see why we cannot relax this rule to the places.googleapis.com domain with the stealth modifier.

Alternatively, we could further tighten it with two rules, although if the URL changes again it will require another edit:
`@@||places.googleapis.com/$rpc/google.maps.places.v1.Places/*$stealth=referrer`
`@@||places.googleapis.com/v1/places*$stealth=referrer`

### Terms

- [x] By submitting this issue, I agree that pull request does not contain private info and all conditions are met

issue.title: [iOS] Google Shopping issue

labels.name: T: Incorrect Blocking
labels.description: Website or app not working properly due to filtering

issue.body:
When I use the shopping category of web google, I get this error when I press the button to access the stores. however if I turn off the AdGuard works perfectly.
1. 
   ![image](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/15856304/15092349/bcb6b8c2-146f-11e6-8dfb-3ddfae38f95d.png)
2. 
   ![image](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/15856304/15092350/c2be9370-146f-11e6-9e80-124f98e46d24.png)
3. 
   ![image](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/15856304/15092351/c8b2d0b6-146f-11e6-8f4a-9f55a4648cee.png)

<details>
Application version: 1.0.5.38
Application lifetime: 2016-04-07 17:46:20 +0000

ID=211 Name="Anti-Adblock Killer | Reek" Version=1.0.1.61 Enabled=YES

comment.body:
1. I open the above-mentioned game.
2. I'm running out of mana to combine objects in the game and I can buy new mana by watching 5-60 seconds one advertising.
3. This particular game sometimes doesn't start the countdown without detecting a click on it.
4. When I click on app Ad,  game opens almost immediately Firefox and the page loads normally due to the rule allowing iOS Safari in the AdGuard Filter - mobile ads.
   - The game does not detect whether I have connected to the Google server or the target domain, hence the default warning screen that the domain is blocked by Peter Lowe filters and probably the Polish one from MajkiIT is better:

> https://github.com/gorhill/ublock/wiki/Strict-blocking

My workaround in uBo:

```adb
||googleadservices.com^$all,_,reason="Reklama",_,important
@@||www.googleadservices.*/aclk?*&adurl=$~3p,badfilter
||www.googleadservices.*/aclk?*&adurl=$~3p,all,urlskip=?adurl +blocked
```

issue.title: fix places.googleapis.com
issue.body:
## Prerequisites

- [x] This is not an ad/bug report;
- [x] My code follows the [guidelines](https://github.com/AdguardTeam/AdguardFilters/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md) and [syntax](https://kb.adguard.com/general/how-to-create-your-own-ad-filters) of this project;
- [x] I have performed a self-review of my own changes;
- [x] My changes do not break web sites, apps and files structure.

## What problem does the pull request fix?

- [x] Website or app doesn't work properly;

## What issue is being fixed?

https://github.com/AdguardTeam/AdguardFilters/issues/233814
https://github.com/AdguardTeam/AdguardFilters/issues/233892
https://github.com/AdguardTeam/AdguardFilters/issues/234157
https://www.jbhifi.com.au/ _(Add any item to cart and then checkout)_

Unable to complete checkout process on various websites due to the Google API being blocked for address confirmation due to hiding the referrer from third parties.

Error output from Google API:
```
{
  "error": {
    "code": 403,
    "message": "Method doesn't allow unregistered callers (callers without established identity). Please use API Key or other form of API consumer identity to call this API.",
    "status": "PERMISSION_DENIED"
  }
}
```

Previous attempts to resolve this worked for specifically chemistwarehouse.com.au with #233892 as the URL was:
`https://places.googleapis.com/$rpc/google.maps.places.v1.Places/AutocompletePlaces`

Then to resolve #234157, it was a different URL:
`https://places.googleapis.com/v1/places:autocomplete`

Now just being on jbhifi.com.au, it's become even more randomised with the following URL:
`https://places.googleapis.com/v1/places/xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx`

---

Currently the rule in **allowlist_stealth.txt** is:
`@@||places.googleapis.com/*autocomplete$stealth=referrer`

So to accommodate all three variants, I've modified the rule to allow it to be broader:
`@@||places.googleapis.com^$stealth=referrer`

As per [Google's API docs](https://developers.google.com/maps/documentation/places/web-service/overview), it looks like this domain is used for accepting HTTP requests for this particular purpose, and I do not see why we cannot relax this rule to the places.googleapis.com domain with the stealth modifier.

Alternatively, we could further tighten it with two rules, although if the URL changes again it will require another edit:
`@@||places.googleapis.com/$rpc/google.maps.places.v1.Places/*$stealth=referrer`
`@@||places.googleapis.com/v1/places*$stealth=referrer`

### Terms

- [x] By submitting this issue, I agree that pull request does not contain private info and all conditions are met

comment.body:
Also, for specifically Address Validation, they have the demo for that as well: https://developers.google.com/maps/documentation/javascript/address-validation/demo Same as above, when trying with 'hide third-party referrers' on, the output is similar:
Websites that utilise these types of Places APIs will not function properly… although, this one uses addressvalidation.googleapis.com, which may also need to be in the allowlist alongside places.googleapis.com.

issue.title: fix places.googleapis.com
issue.body:
## Prerequisites

- [x] This is not an ad/bug report;
- [x] My code follows the [guidelines](https://github.com/AdguardTeam/AdguardFilters/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md) and [syntax](https://kb.adguard.com/general/how-to-create-your-own-ad-filters) of this project;
- [x] I have performed a self-review of my own changes;
- [x] My changes do not break web sites, apps and files structure.

## What problem does the pull request fix?

- [x] Website or app doesn't work properly;

## What issue is being fixed?

https://github.com/AdguardTeam/AdguardFilters/issues/233814
https://github.com/AdguardTeam/AdguardFilters/issues/233892
https://github.com/AdguardTeam/AdguardFilters/issues/234157
https://www.jbhifi.com.au/ _(Add any item to cart and then checkout)_

Unable to complete checkout process on various websites due to the Google API being blocked for address confirmation due to hiding the referrer from third parties.

Error output from Google API:
```
{
  "error": {
    "code": 403,
    "message": "Method doesn't allow unregistered callers (callers without established identity). Please use API Key or other form of API consumer identity to call this API.",
    "status": "PERMISSION_DENIED"
  }
}
```

Previous attempts to resolve this worked for specifically chemistwarehouse.com.au with #233892 as the URL was:
`https://places.googleapis.com/$rpc/google.maps.places.v1.Places/AutocompletePlaces`

Then to resolve #234157, it was a different URL:
`https://places.googleapis.com/v1/places:autocomplete`

Now just being on jbhifi.com.au, it's become even more randomised with the following URL:
`https://places.googleapis.com/v1/places/xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx`

---

Currently the rule in **allowlist_stealth.txt** is:
`@@||places.googleapis.com/*autocomplete$stealth=referrer`

So to accommodate all three variants, I've modified the rule to allow it to be broader:
`@@||places.googleapis.com^$stealth=referrer`

As per [Google's API docs](https://developers.google.com/maps/documentation/places/web-service/overview), it looks like this domain is used for accepting HTTP requests for this particular purpose, and I do not see why we cannot relax this rule to the places.googleapis.com domain with the stealth modifier.

Alternatively, we could further tighten it with two rules, although if the URL changes again it will require another edit:
`@@||places.googleapis.com/$rpc/google.maps.places.v1.Places/*$stealth=referrer`
`@@||places.googleapis.com/v1/places*$stealth=referrer`

### Terms

- [x] By submitting this issue, I agree that pull request does not contain private info and all conditions are met

comment.body: Hang on @Alex-302, figured something out. For Autocomplete, try the demo form with the 'hide third-party referrers' on: https://developers.google.com/maps/documentation/javascript/examples/places-autocomplete-addressform When you click on an address suggestion, the form does not work and you should see something like this.

comment.body:
Okay, looks like:

1. [autocomplete is a Places API](https://developers.google.com/maps/documentation/places/web-service/place-autocomplete) that is exclusively using `https://places.googleapis.com/v1/places:autocomplete`; and
2. I am not too sure why the checkout experience on jbhifi.com.au has suddenly switched methods overnight to `themeapis.jbhifi.com.au`, and I had really bad timing to report this. 

The current rule in **allowlist_stealth.txt** covers the API url variations at the moment, so I reckon we close this. If there's a website misbehaving again now that I have a better understanding of how Google API functions for these shopping checkouts, I'll report it via the web tool first.

This issue has been automatically marked as stale because it has been open for 7 days with no activity.
Unfortunately, our resources are limited and we can not always take the time to respond to requests about websites that are not popular enough. The issue will be closed if no further activity occurs.
Please note, that AdGuard Filters is an open-source project that is used by different content blockers and we welcome contributions. If you have some technical knowledge about how websites work, you are welcome to create a pull request following [these instructions](https://github.com/AdguardTeam/AdguardFilters/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md).

issue.title: fix places.googleapis.com
issue.body:
## Prerequisites

- [x] This is not an ad/bug report;
- [x] My code follows the [guidelines](https://github.com/AdguardTeam/AdguardFilters/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md) and [syntax](https://kb.adguard.com/general/how-to-create-your-own-ad-filters) of this project;
- [x] I have performed a self-review of my own changes;
- [x] My changes do not break web sites, apps and files structure.

## What problem does the pull request fix?

- [x] Website or app doesn't work properly;

## What issue is being fixed?

https://github.com/AdguardTeam/AdguardFilters/issues/233814
https://github.com/AdguardTeam/AdguardFilters/issues/233892
https://github.com/AdguardTeam/AdguardFilters/issues/234157
https://www.jbhifi.com.au/ _(Add any item to cart and then checkout)_

Unable to complete checkout process on various websites due to the Google API being blocked for address confirmation due to hiding the referrer from third parties.

Error output from Google API:
```
{
  "error": {
    "code": 403,
    "message": "Method doesn't allow unregistered callers (callers without established identity). Please use API Key or other form of API consumer identity to call this API.",
    "status": "PERMISSION_DENIED"
  }
}
```

Previous attempts to resolve this worked for specifically chemistwarehouse.com.au with #233892 as the URL was:
`https://places.googleapis.com/$rpc/google.maps.places.v1.Places/AutocompletePlaces`

Then to resolve #234157, it was a different URL:
`https://places.googleapis.com/v1/places:autocomplete`

Now just being on jbhifi.com.au, it's become even more randomised with the following URL:
`https://places.googleapis.com/v1/places/xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx`

---

Currently the rule in **allowlist_stealth.txt** is:
`@@||places.googleapis.com/*autocomplete$stealth=referrer`

So to accommodate all three variants, I've modified the rule to allow it to be broader:
`@@||places.googleapis.com^$stealth=referrer`

As per [Google's API docs](https://developers.google.com/maps/documentation/places/web-service/overview), it looks like this domain is used for accepting HTTP requests for this particular purpose, and I do not see why we cannot relax this rule to the places.googleapis.com domain with the stealth modifier.

Alternatively, we could further tighten it with two rules, although if the URL changes again it will require another edit:
`@@||places.googleapis.com/$rpc/google.maps.places.v1.Places/*$stealth=referrer`
`@@||places.googleapis.com/v1/places*$stealth=referrer`

### Terms

- [x] By submitting this issue, I agree that pull request does not contain private info and all conditions are met

comment.body:
> Error without AdGuard

With that one, that one is due to the API key not being valid (visit the same link in browser to see 403 error reason).

When AdGuard blocks it, it is due to the domain not being the right one for the API (e.g. configured for example.com, but AdGuard rewrites it for places.googleapis.com and makes it invalid).

issue.body:
### Issue URL (Incorrect Blocking) [https://www.genealogybank.com/](https://adguardteam.github.io/AnonymousRedirect/redirect.html?url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.genealogybank.com%2F) ### Comment > This webpage (geneologybank) won't load with Adguard for PC enabled. It works normally once Adguard is disabled. It also works normally on multiple Samsung mobile devices with Adguard for android enabled. This issue only happens on Windows PC. It also works normally with just Adguard VPN enabled on PC, just not the actual ad blocker program from Adguard. It also works normally if other ad blocking browser extensions are enabled like Adblock Plus and Ghostery. The same issue happens on Edge with Adguard for PC enabled, however on Edge the website loads normally with the Adguard extension enabled. It seems to only be the Adguard for Windows PC Desktop that is conflicting with this website for some reason. ### Screenshots

issue.title: fix places.googleapis.com
issue.body:
## Prerequisites

- [x] This is not an ad/bug report;
- [x] My code follows the [guidelines](https://github.com/AdguardTeam/AdguardFilters/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md) and [syntax](https://kb.adguard.com/general/how-to-create-your-own-ad-filters) of this project;
- [x] I have performed a self-review of my own changes;
- [x] My changes do not break web sites, apps and files structure.

## What problem does the pull request fix?

- [x] Website or app doesn't work properly;

## What issue is being fixed?

https://github.com/AdguardTeam/AdguardFilters/issues/233814
https://github.com/AdguardTeam/AdguardFilters/issues/233892
https://github.com/AdguardTeam/AdguardFilters/issues/234157
https://www.jbhifi.com.au/ _(Add any item to cart and then checkout)_

Unable to complete checkout process on various websites due to the Google API being blocked for address confirmation due to hiding the referrer from third parties.

Error output from Google API:
```
{
  "error": {
    "code": 403,
    "message": "Method doesn't allow unregistered callers (callers without established identity). Please use API Key or other form of API consumer identity to call this API.",
    "status": "PERMISSION_DENIED"
  }
}
```

Previous attempts to resolve this worked for specifically chemistwarehouse.com.au with #233892 as the URL was:
`https://places.googleapis.com/$rpc/google.maps.places.v1.Places/AutocompletePlaces`

Then to resolve #234157, it was a different URL:
`https://places.googleapis.com/v1/places:autocomplete`

Now just being on jbhifi.com.au, it's become even more randomised with the following URL:
`https://places.googleapis.com/v1/places/xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx`

---

Currently the rule in **allowlist_stealth.txt** is:
`@@||places.googleapis.com/*autocomplete$stealth=referrer`

So to accommodate all three variants, I've modified the rule to allow it to be broader:
`@@||places.googleapis.com^$stealth=referrer`

As per [Google's API docs](https://developers.google.com/maps/documentation/places/web-service/overview), it looks like this domain is used for accepting HTTP requests for this particular purpose, and I do not see why we cannot relax this rule to the places.googleapis.com domain with the stealth modifier.

Alternatively, we could further tighten it with two rules, although if the URL changes again it will require another edit:
`@@||places.googleapis.com/$rpc/google.maps.places.v1.Places/*$stealth=referrer`
`@@||places.googleapis.com/v1/places*$stealth=referrer`

### Terms

- [x] By submitting this issue, I agree that pull request does not contain private info and all conditions are met

comment.body:
> Need steps to reproduce on https://www.jbhifi.com.au/

I made this request when the online reporting tool was not working and couldn't create a new issue (https://github.com/AdguardTeam/ReportsWebApp/issues/429)

It looks like they have already changed their method on checkout, as I can now see they instead are using their own first-party solution combined with Shopify: `https://themeapis.jbhifi.com.au/address/checkout/search`

Bare with me a moment and I'll find another website that uses the new Google API for address validation.
